data.title: China telling AI executives avoid travel to US over security concerns: report
data.description:
Beijing is telling artificial intelligence researchers and entrepreneurs to avoid visits to the U.S., The Wall Street Journal reported Saturday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The Journal said officials are worried that Chinese AI experts traveling could disclose confidential information on the country’s progress with AI.

The Journal quoted people in the tech issue as saying there is no outright travel ban but there is guidance from Chinese officials in Beijing, Shanghai, and the province of Zhejiang. Zhejiang is home to Alibaba (BABA) and DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK).

The people said officials there are discouraging local AI executives and those in other sensitive industries to not take nonurgent trips to the US and its allies, The Journal said. Executives who do travel are told to disclose their plans before departing and to brief officials on their activities abroad when they return, the Journal added.

DeepSeek founder Liang Wenfeng declined an invitation to last month’s AI summit in Paris, the report said, citing people familiar with the matter.

Some of the people reportedly said another AI startup’s founder cancelled a planned trip to the U.S. after instructions from the Chinese government, according to the report.

China’s embassy in Washington, D.C. did not immediately respond to a request for comment by Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Trending stocks as market closes volatile final week of February
data.description:
Wall Street faced off rising geopolitical tensions, trade war signals and economic slowdown in a wild week that capped off February.

The S&P 500 ended the week 0.75% lower and closed the month around 0.7% down. The Dow Jones Industrial Average had a more positive week, closing 1.4% higher, while the Nasdaq Composite was up 1.6%.

Analysts and traders have projected volatile waters ahead for the market after U.S. President Donald Trump got into a shouting match with his Ukrainian counterpart, Volodymyr Zelenskiy.

Meanwhile, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent welcomed Mexico’s proposal to match U.S. tariffs on China and urged the same from Canada. A slew of tariffs from both countries could signal more unpredictability ahead.

Consumer spending appeared soft as well, with January core personal consumption expenditures price index rising 2.6% Y/Y and inflation-adjusted consumer spending down 0.5%, the biggest monthly decline in nearly four years.

Amid these shocks in the market, these were some of the trending stocks:

Chinese electric vehicle stocks advanced over the week despite concerns that a trade war between the U.S. and China could darken the sector. XPeng (XPEV) led the pack with +18.8% gain, while Li Auto (LI) rose +16.2%.

Super Micro Computer (SMCI) came in spotlight as the company filed its "delinquent" financial reports with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission just ahead of its deadline. Shares initially jumped on the news, but declined 23% for the week as investors reacted negatively to the 10-K filing.

B Riley Financial (RILY) soared 73% as the company regained compliance with Nasdaq listing rule.

Cryptocurrency-exposed stocks slide as Bitcoin (BTC-USD) tumbled below $85,000, paring the gains seen since Donald Trump’s election to the White House. MicroStrategy (MSTR) was down -23% for the week, CleanSpark (CLSK) fell 13.7%, Hut 8 (HUT) -10.9%, Coinbase Global (COIN) -9.7%, Galaxy Digital Holdings (OTCPK:BRPHF) -4.6%, Bakkt Holdings (BKKT) -12%, Riot Platforms (RIOT) -12.4%, MARA Holdings (MARA) -4.6%, HIVE Digital Technologies (HIVE) -17% and Bit Digital (BTBT) dropped -8.5%.

Eli Lilly (LLY) was in focus as the drugmaker expanded its weight loss drug offerings, launching new 7.5 mg and 10 mg single-dose vial formats of Zepbound (tirzepatide) at $499 through its direct-to-consumer platform, LillyDirect, nearly 23% cheaper than its injector pen versions.

Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) shares tumbled after-hours on Monday after the telehealth company reported fourth-quarter financial results and said it will soon stop selling some compound weight-loss drugs.

SoundHound (SOUN) advanced 8.4% after its fourth quarter results beat estimates. The company also raised its full-year 2025 revenue outlook to a range of $157M-$177M (midpoint: $167M), up from the prior $155M-$175M range and above the $165.27M consensus.

Applovin (APP) fell 20.9% after Fuzzy Panda and Culper Research each released short reports against the company.

NetApp (NTAP) dropped 19.7% after the tech firm released its third quarter fiscal 2025 financial results, with revenue falling below expectations.

Rocket Lab (RKLB) shares fell 18% at market open on Friday after report the company reported fourth quarter earnings and issued an Q1 sales outlook that fell below expectations.

Tesla (TSLA) erased 13% after data from the European Automobile Manufacturers Association showed that the EV maker’s sales in Europe dropped 45% in January. Shares of the Elon Musk-led company are down about 40% from their late 2024 high.

data.title: Real estate stocks stand out as winners in February despite a rise in bear sentiment
data.description:
S&P 500 real estate stocks closed February ~5% higher on average, while the major market averages ended their journey in red amid a surge in bearish sentiment.

Bearish sentiment among individual levels surged to the highest level in 2-1/2 years this past week as the tech and momentum trades showed some vulnerability.

The benchmark S&P 500 concluded February down by 1.4%, driven by uncertainties stemming from President Donald Trump's tariff moves and hot inflation data.

Investors are selling off risk assets, with the risk appetite on a decline since Trump ramped up tariff threats for Canada, Mexico, China, and even the European Union.

The second month of the year has traditionally been a weak one for markets, but the defensive sectors and bond-sensitive stocks have recently performed better than other areas of the stock market.

"The rotation from equities to bonds continues apace, as evidenced by the drop in the 10-year yield. The 10-year is down from 4.6% at the inauguration to 4.2% today – we believe it has further to fall," Alex King, investing group leader of Cestrian Capital Research, told Seeking Alpha.

Real estate stocks have been on a gaining spree amid falling long-term yields and mortgage rates. The Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLRE) saw outflows worth $10.56M on Monday, but logged $32.17M of inflows on Wednesday.

For the last week of the month, the S&P 500 Real Estate Index Sector (SP500-60) rose 2.13% to 271.05, while the accompanying The Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLRE) added 2.49% to close at 43.15.

The Dow Jones Equity All REIT Total Return Index (NASDAQ:REIT:IND) gained 1.92% on a weekly basis to finish at 2,730.07.

The FTSE Nareit All Equity REITs index (INDEXFTSE: FNER), which includes all tax-qualified REITs with more than 50% of assets in qualifying real estate other than mortgages secured by real property that also meet minimum size and liquidity criteria, advanced 2.18% W/W to 796.74.

Meanwhile, S&P 500 closed the week 0.98% lower at 5,954.43.

Seeking Alpha analysts continue to grade XLRE as Buy. But the Quant Rating system changed its stand on the ETF to Buy from Hold today, with a focus on Momentum.

Optimism

Real estate brokerage leaders reported record-high optimism in profitability, transactions, and market growth, the latest Delta Real Estate Leadership Survey said.

"After several years of uncertainty, brokerages are navigating market changes with renewed optimism and strategic focus. They see opportunities in technology, talent acquisition, and overall market share growth. Despite the challenges 2025 will present, the brokerage leaders we surveyed are bullish on success," said Michael Minard, CEO of Delta Media Group.

Majority of the real estate investors are planning to grow their portfolios and invest significantly in property improvements in 2025, another survey from property management software provider RentRedi showed.

2025 housing market will be slightly better than 2024 in terms of originations and sales, said Ryan McKeveny, the managing director of mortgage and real estate at Zelman & Associates, at the Housing Economic Summit by the housing market and industry data information service provider, HousingWire.

Company News

  • Matterport (MTTR) and Costar (CSGP) said the HSR waiting period for their transaction has expired, though the companies are still working with the Federal Trade Commission. The companies continue to work "cooperatively" with the FTC in its review of the transaction and expect that the deal will be completed in the Q1.
  • Invitation Homes (INVH) reported Q4 FFO of $0.47 and revenue of $659.13M (+5.6% Y/Y), in-line with consensus. Separately, INVH announced the promotion of Timothy Lobner to executive vice president and COO post, effective March 2.
  • Federal Realty Investment Trust (FRT) acquired Del Monte Shopping Center for $123.5M.
  • Extra Space Storage (EXR) delivered Q4 earnings and revenue that exceeded the Wall Street consensus. Meanwhile, the company issued 2025 guidance that assumes a slowly improving operating environment.
  • Simon Property Group (SPG), Shopify and Leap commerce platform for physical retail are collaborating to help ecommerce brands open brick-and-mortar locations across Simon's portfolio of properties.
  • American Tower (AMT) Q4 earnings missed the average analyst estimate as operating expenses rose from the prior quarter, exceeding the Wall Street consensus. Guidance for 2025 AFFO per share also disappointed.
  • Public Storage (PSA) issued 2025 guidance incorporated the estimated impact from the Los Angeles wildfires, following Q4 earnings that missed the average analyst estimate.
  • SBA Communications (SBAC) declared $1.11/share quarterly dividend, 13.3% increase from the prior dividend of $0.98. Separately, SBAC posted Q4 FFO of $3.47, which beats by $0.19, and revenue of $693.7M (+2.8% Y/Y), which beats by $11.3M.
  • Realty Income (O) delivered 2025 guidance that was lower than the average analyst estimate and posted Q4 earnings in-line with the consensus estimate.

Winner & Losers

Residential REITs stood out as the winner among the S&P 500 REIT subsectors this week, adding 4.55% from last week on average. Most of the gains were driven by Invitation Homes (INVH) (+6.65% W/W) and Essex Property Trust (ESS) (+5.71% W/W)

Health Care REITs followed, advancing 3.99% on a weekly basis. Alexandria Real Estate Equities (ARE) was the second best performer for the week among the individual S&P 500 real estate stocks (+7.00% W/W).

American Tower (AMT) led the pack with a 7.35% increase from last week, despite an earnings miss as the telecom tower REIT shifts focus to data centers.

Hotel & Resort REITs was the only REIT subsector to end in red, with Host Hotels & Resorts (HST) recording 1.29% of weekly losses. Digital Realty Trust (DLR) (-5.47% W/W) and Extra Space Storage (EXR) (-2.17% W/W) topped the list of S&P 500 real estate losers.

The S&P 500 Real Estate Management & Development index bounced back to green after three consecutive weeks of losses, advancing 2.26% as mortgage rates hit a two-month low.

For the broader real estate sector, Avalon GloboCare (ALBT) (+33.76% W/W), Medical Properties Trust (MPW) (+21.15% W/W), and Diversified Healthcare Trust (DHC) (+14.11% W/W) were the top gainers of the week.

Here is a look at the subsector performances for the week:

data.title: AppLovin selloff follows significant insider sales
data.description:
Shares of AppLovin (NASDAQ:APP) plunged ~22% this week, extending an ~18% drop in the prior week after two short seller reports from Fuzzy Panda Research and Culper Research alleged fraudulent activity related to the company’s AXON advertising software.

AppLovin’s (NASDAQ:APP) recent selloff contrasts its meteoric rise in 2024 when shares of the Palo Alto, California-based firm surged over 700% even as the company insiders locked in much of those gains through some of the notable sales last year.

However, according to data from OpenInsider, no APP insiders bought company shares in 2024, which, according to Seeking Alpha analyst Hataf Capital, represented a major bearish signal. “The fact that no insider purchases were recorded during the whole year of intense selling activity further reinforces the bearish signal,” Hataf Capital wrote.

The analyst pointed to more than $1.6B share sale by KKR (KKR) in November when the early AppLovin (NASDAQ:APP) investor fully divested its company stake. “This large-scale exit by a sophisticated institutional investor that likely has deep insight into the company's operations and prospects is particularly concerning,” the analyst argued at the time.

However, technology chief Vasily (Basil) Shikin’s $189.5M worth of share sale, which cut its ownership stake by ~11% just before KKR’s divestment, was also significant, Hataf Capital wrote.

“The timing and size of these sales from the company's technical leader, given AppLovin's heavy emphasis on its AI-driven AXON technology, raise questions about the sustainability of the company's competitive advantage,” the analyst added. Piling on to insider sales, co-founder and CEO Adam Foroughi sold more than $72M worth of APP stock across four transactions in 2024.

While Hataf Capital reasoned that significant insider selling can be common after a sharp stock price appreciation, the analyst cautioned that “the concentration and magnitude of these sales in November 2024 far exceed typical profit-taking behavior.”

However, both Foroughi and Shikin remain very much invested in the company, directly owning ~2.8M and ~3.5M APP shares, respectively, according to the latest SEC filings.

data.title: Double-down or fold? Investors eye the best strategies for Macau casino stocks
data.description:
The most recent development for the Macau casino sector was very positive. Macau gross gaming revenue increased by 6.8% year-over-year in February to 19.74 billion patacas ($2.5 billion), according to data from the Gaming Inspection and Coordination Bureau. The GGR tally smashed the consensus expectation for a rise of just 0.8% and kept alive the bull thesis that a full recovery is still in the works.

Gross gaming revenue was boosted by the last few days of the Chinese Lunar New Year holiday. After two months, Macau GGR is up 0.5% from the level seen in 2025 or roughly 79% of the pre-pandemic level seen in 2019.

Despite the strong month, analysts have been cautious on the Macau casino sector. Citi recently lowered its Macau gaming revenue forecast for 2025, with GGR now seen growing by 3% for the full year, instead of the initial forecast for 7% growth. "We believe the implementation of US tariffs against China on 1 February has dampened gaming demand towards the end of the CNY Golden Week, and we expect this impact to continue throughout most of 1H25. Coupled with the relatively high base in 1H, 1H25 Macau GGR is expected to fall 1% year-on-year," read a note from the firm.

Separately, CreditSights warned recently that GGR per visitor is being held back by the minimal presence of junket-sourced VIPs in the market following the near collapse of the junket industry in late 2022. The firm also highlighted that visitation from affluent Chinese cities such as Shanghai, Beijing, Guangdong, Jiangsu, Zhejiang, and Tianjin has already recovered to pre-COVID levels in an indication that further growth may have to come from other regions.

In general, the rising threat of a trade war and tariff battle is seen as having the potential to lower the spending power of high-value mainland Chinese players, particularly factory owners with interests in export sectors. Looking at market data, past confrontations between the U.S. and China have negatively affected the stock prices of U.S.-based Macau casino operators.

Also of importance, President Trump urged the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States to impose investment restrictions on nations classified as foreign adversaries. Since Hong Kong and Macau are Chinese territories, they are also encompassed in this category, along with China in general, Cuba, North Korea, Russia, and Venezuela. Of course, three of Macau’s six casino operators (MGM China, Sands China, and Wynn Macau) are subsidiaries of U.S.-based companies. However, another wildcard is that Las Vegas Sands' (NYSE:LVS) majority shareholders Miriam Adelson has close ties to President Trump.

Macau-related casino stocks: Wynn Macau (OTCPK:WYNMF) (OTCPK:WYNMY), Wynn Resorts (WYNN), Sands China (OTCPK:SCHYY) (OTCPK:SCHYF), Las Vegas Sands (NYSE:LVS), MGM China (OTCPK:MCHVF), (OTCPK:MCHVY), MGM Resorts (MGM), Galaxy Entertainment (OTCPK:GXYEF), SJM Holdings (OTCPK:SJMHF), (OTCPK:SJMHY), Melco Resorts & Entertainment (NASDAQ:MLCO), and Studio City International (NYSE:MSC).

data.title: Insider trades: Coca Cola, Walmart among notable names this week
data.description:
A weekly overview of insider trading, highlighting significant purchases and sales by investors, directors, and executives. The transactions occurred between February 24, 2025, and February 28, 2025.

  • Coca Cola’s (NYSE:KO) CEO James Quincey sold 145,435 shares of the company at $71.01 per share for a total value of about $10M. Quincey currently holds 342,546 shares in the company. Meanwhile, Monica Howard Douglas, executive vice president at the company, sold 5,565 shares of the company for $70.84 per share for a total value of $394,231. She now holds 37,725 shares in the firm.
  • Walmart’s (NYSE:WMT) executive vice president Christopher Nicholas disclosed through a filing that he sold 17,600 shares of the company for $94.42 per share for a total value of $1.66M. Nicholas now holds about 348,357 Walmart shares.
  • Gary Coombe, CEO of Procter & Gamble’s (NYSE:PG) grooming business, sold 18,000 shares of the common stock for a price of $171.66 per share for a total worth of $3M. Coombe now directly holds about 22,050 shares of the company.
  • McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) chief people officer Tiffanie Boyd offloaded 1,000 shares of the firm for around $309.27 per share for a total value of $309,270. Boyd now holds around 5,563 shares in the company.
  • Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) revealed in a filing that CEO and Chairman David Solomon sold 2,415 shares of the company for $622.15 per share for a total value of $1.5M. Following the sale, Solomon now holds 115,468 shares of the company.
  • IBM’s (NYSE:IBM) Director David Farr bought 1,200 shares this week for $249 per share for a total value of $298,800. He now owns 8,258 shares in the firm.
  • Carmine Di Sibio, director at Prudential Financial (NYSE:PRU) disclosed a transaction this week. Di Sibio purchased 200 shares for $111.04 each, amounting to $22,208. Di Sibio now holds 200 shares in the firm.
  • Oaktree Speciality (NASDAQ:OCSL) Lending’s director Phyllis Caldwell purchased 2,500 shares of the company for $15.83 per share for a total value of $39,575. Caldwell now owns 16,000 shares in the company.

data.title: DeepSeek claims theoretical daily cost-profit ration of 545%
data.description:
DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK) on Saturday claimed a theoretical daily cost-profit ratio of 545% related to its V3 and R1 models, but it warned that actual revenue would be much lower.

The AI startup’s post on GitLab was the first time it disclosed any profit margin information from “inference” tasks, which is the stage that involves trained AI models making predictions and doing tasks after they have been trained.

DeepSeek said in the post that, assuming the hourly rent for a H800 is $2, the total daily inference costs for its V3 and R1 models would be $87,072. It said, in contrast, theoretical daily revenue generated by the models is $562,027. That would total more than $205M revenue in a year.

H800 chips are produced by Nvidia (NVDA).

However, DeepSeek said actual revenue would be “substantially lower” because the cost of using its V3 model is less than the R1 model, just some services are monetized, and developers pay less for off-peak usage. '

DeepSeek emerged in late January when it released an AI model that reportedly outperformed Microsoft-backed (MSFT) OpenAI in many third-party tests and sparked debate about American dominance in the AI Sector.

data.title: Insurers jump, crypto-tied stocks slide in week's biggest financial movers
data.description:
Insurance companies dominated the biggest weekly winners among financial stocks, while crypto-linked firms led the steepest declines overall.

For the week ended Feb. 28, the Financial Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLF) drove up 2.8%, compared with the S&P 500's 1.0% dip. In addition to the flurry of corporate earnings reports, much of this week's price action centered on uncertainties stemming from Trump's tariff moves and hot inflation data.

Root (ROOT) was the week's biggest gainer of any financial stock (with market cap over $2B), surging 35%, after the auto insurance platform achieved its first-ever net income profitability;

Real estate investment manager Bridge Investment Group Holdings (BRDG) gapped up 33% on the back of an agreement to sell itself to Apollo Global Management for $1.5B;

Goosehead Insurance (GSHD), which during the week posted better-than-expected Q4 headline results, advanced 16%;

Annuities provider Jackson Financial (JXN) gained 12%; and

Erie Indemnity Company (ERIE) rounded out the five biggest financial winners, accelerating 12%, after posting mixed Q4 results.

Conversely, crypto miners Cleanspark (CLSK) and Riot Platforms (RIOT) fell the most of any financial stock, sliding 14% and 11%, respectively, as the price of bitcoin (BTC-USD) dropped double-digits on the week;

Taking the third slot, Hong Kong-based Futu Holdings (FUTU) retreated 10%;

Coinbase Global (COIN) shed 8.4% even after the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Thursday dismissed its civil 2023 lawsuit against the crypto exchange;

AI-powered lending platform Upstart Holdings (UPST) dipped 7.1%.

data.title: Notable analyst calls this week: Nike, Lucid and Block stocks among top picks
data.description:
Major U.S. indexes closed higher on Friday, after the week saw headlines including semiconductor giant Nvidia's (NVDA) earnings report, more tariff-related moves and a heated argument between President Donald Trump and Ukrainian counterpart Volodymyr Zelenskyy.

For the week, Nasdaq (COMP:IND) declined nearly 4%, while Dow (DJI) fell 0.5%.

Wall Street had a slew of upgrades and downgrades from analysts. Here are some of the major calls for the week:

Jefferies upgrades Nike, says ‘now is the right time’ to buy shares

Jefferies upgraded Nike (NYSE:NKE) to a Buy from Hold, with analyst Randal Konik saying that now is the right time to aggressively buy shares of the sportswear giant.

“Nike’s brand ubiquity and global distribution advantage should allow it to outgrow the market – we project a ~7% CAGR vs ~3% for the street,” Konik said and hiked PT for the stock by 53%.

Konik further added that he expects a V-shaped margin/EPS recovery in FY27 well ahead of the consensus estimates.

Conagra downgraded at Goldman Sachs on supply constraints

Goldman Sachs downgraded Conagra Brands (NYSE:CAG) to Neutral from Buy on what analysts believe is an elongated path of improvement for Conagra, with less visibility.

The brokerage, which set a PT of $26, noted certain continued headwinds tied to increasing competition, higher promotions, and rising input costs. GS also believes recent supply constraint issues delay the sales improvement story for Conagra Brands.

Twilio upgraded on improved execution

Morgan Stanley upgraded Twilio (NYSE:TWLO) to Overweight from Equal-Weight, citing improving execution.

“We are increasingly confident in crediting TWLO's execution toward double-digit growth and operating margin outperformance on back of increased cross-sell, supported by our recent checks,” analyst Meta Marshall and upped PT to $160 from $144.

Block receives upgrades from MS, BMO

Block (NYSE:XYZ) was in focus after the fintech stock was upgraded by Morgan Stanley and BMO.

BMO Capital Markets upgraded the stock to Outperform from Market Perform as analyst Rufus Hone sees the potential for investor sentiment to improve through 2025 as Cash App/Square gross profit growth accelerates.

Similarly, Morgan Stanley upgraded it to Equalweight from Underweight and see the risk/reward for XYZ as more balanced.

Lucid downgraded after quarterly results, CEO transition

Lucid Group (NASDAQ:LCID) was downgraded to Under Perform by BofA after the company issued its Q4 earnings report and announced a CEO transition.

The brokerage thinks the departure of Lucid founder Peter Rawlinson is much more consequential than perhaps understood by the market.

"We note that LCID in prior public filings stated that Rawlinson was a key member, andthat it is highly dependent on the services of Peter Rawlinson, who is a significantinfluence on and driver of our technology development and business plan."

BofA believes risks are piling up for Rivian (NASDAQ:RIVN) and downgraded the stock to Underperform from Neutral. The brokerage cut 23% off its PT down to $10 and said one of the more significant risks to Rivian is increased competition as new SUVs/and crossovers enter the EV market next year and into 2027 from Lucid (NASDAQ:LCID), Chevy (GM), and Ford (F).

Chinese e-commerce and cloud service giant Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) was included in Benchmark's Best Ideas list. The firm noted that as AI adoption increases in China, driven by the breakout of DeepSeek, BABA is best positioned to capitalize on the growth.

Morgan Stanley upgraded Intuit (NASDAQ:INTU) to Overweight from Equalweight, as the company's momentum in the small business sector builds, its efficiency initiatives start yielding, and its conservative H2 2025 outlook bodes well for positive earnings revisions in the near term.

data.title: Bulls vs. Bears: Nvidia Q4 results divide SA community
data.description:
Nvidia Corporation (NASDAQ:NVDA) was the subject of a wide range of ratings from Seeking Alpha analysts as shares of the semiconductor giant fell this week, even after its Q4 2024 financials and full-year outlook released on Wednesday exceeded Street forecasts.

Here are some excerpts from the most bullish and bearish Seeking Alpha articles on the Jensen Huang-led company published after the company’s latest earnings report.

The Bulls

“Nvidia delivered 77.9% revenue growth in Q4, with 93.4% growth in the data center market,” wrote SA analyst Hunter Wolf Research in “Nvidia Q4: Blackwell Production Ramp-Up Strong.” “Nvidia’s data center business has outperformed AMD (AMD) and Intel (INTC) in recent years. It is evident that Intel is struggling for growth, and AMD is playing a catch-up, but far behind.”

“Nvidia's data center sales surged 94% YOY, and the company consistently beats EPS estimates, making its current valuation attractive for a market leader,” added SA Investing Group Leader Victor Dergunov in “Nvidia: The King Of AI Is Still A Strong Buy.” “Nvidia Corporation's strong financial performance, including top and bottom-line beats and better-than-expected guidance, continues to support a bullish outlook despite market skepticism.”

“Nvidia projects $5 billion in automotive AI revenue this fiscal year, nearly tripling last year’s sales,” noted SA analyst Deep Value Investing in “Nvidia: The Shift Towards Reasoning Models And The Importance Of Hedging.” “I believe Nvidia Corporation’s record Q4 data center revenue and Blackwell AI's rapid adoption confirm the high demand for AI infrastructure.”

The Bears

“Despite strong earnings, Nvidia's guidance indicates slower revenue growth and increasing operating expenses, making NVDA stock a poor long-term investment,” cautioned SA Investing Group Leader The Value Portfolio in “Nvidia And The Law Of Large Numbers.” “Major competitors like Apple, Intel, and Amazon are ramping up efforts, posing significant threats to Nvidia's market share in the long term.”

“Despite impressive Q4 results, Nvidia's gross margins fell below expectations, and management anticipates further declines due to the Blackwell architecture ramp-up,” opined analyst Noah's Arc Capital Management in “Nvidia Q4 Earnings Review: Losing On AI Inference.” “Nvidia's valuation remains high compared to sector medians, and shrinking margins could lead to P/E multiple compression, presenting downside risks.”
